channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Why evolving cyber threats mean small businesses are ransomware targets
item.description: Dark Matter’s powerful fictional short film based on real events, Butterfly, goes inside the experience of a small business as its world is turned upside down by ransomware.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Fiscal Service using death data to reduce improper payments
item.description: Tim Gribben, the commissioner of the Bureau of the Fiscal Service, said the bureau refined and reframed its progress statement for the first time since 2018. The post Fiscal Service using death data to reduce improper payments first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Google says it will stop linking to New Zealand news if a law passes forcing it to pay for content
item.description: Google says it will stop linking to New Zealand news content and will reverse its support of local media outlets if the government passes a law forcing tech companies to pay for articles displayed on their platforms.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The creepy yet helpful humanoid robot ready to move into your home
item.description: A robot that stands more than 5 feet tall and weighs 66 pounds is well-equipped for household tasks, like carrying up to 44 pounds during its run time of two to four hours.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Let your iPhone read aloud what you can't see
item.description: The new Reader Mode in iOS 18’s Magnifier app is here to help iPhone users read something small or in poor lighting. All you have to do is snap a picture.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google, it’s time to kill CAPTCHAS
item.description: Are you a robot? Google really, really wants to know. The answer to this question is demanded of web users 200 million times a day via CAPTCHAs — “Completely Automated Public Turing test to tell Computers and Humans Apart,” a system owned and operated by Google.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Biden signs bill cutting environmental reviews for semiconductor industry
item.description: President Biden on Wednesday signed legislation exempting semiconductor manufacturing facilities from environmental reviews — despite objections from some Democrats and green activists who have expressed concerns about the potential for toxic waste.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, The Hill

item.title: Cyber Leaders Exchange 2024: CISA’s Jeff Greene on challenging the status quo
item.description: The new executive assistant director for cybersecurity at CISA details how sharing threat intelligence more broadly pays off. The post Cyber Leaders Exchange 2024: CISA’s Jeff Greene on challenging the status quo first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Fix for BGP routing insecurity ‘plagued by software vulnerabilities’ of its own, researchers find
item.description: Resource Public Key Infrastructure (RPKI) is not yet the simple fix for the security weaknesses of the internet’s Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) many in the communications industry think it is, a team of German researchers has warned.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: CISA faces challenges to sharing cyber threat information
item.description: The IG said CISA made some good improvements to the Automated Indicator Sharing program in recent years. The post CISA faces challenges to sharing cyber threat information first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: Tesla posts first quarterly increase in deliveries, but shares slump with investors hoping for more
item.description: Low interest financing, sweet lease deals, price cuts and free charging boosted Tesla’s global deliveries in the third quarter, the first increase this year for the electric vehicle maker.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: iPhone's satellite messaging: A lifeline in Hurricane Helene's aftermath
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how iOS 18’s satellite messaging helps isolated residents seek help in the aftermath of Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Australia's online dating industry adopts code of conduct to keep users safer
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Details from New Mexico's lawsuit against Snap show site failed to act on reports of sextortion
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico's attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: PyTorch library makes models faster and smaller
item.description: The PyTorch Foundation, makers of the PyTorch machine learning framework, has launched torchao, a PyTorch native library that makes models faster and smaller by leveraging low-bit dtypes, sparsity, and quantization.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: New Jersey offshore wind farm clears big federal hurdle amid environmental concerns
item.description: SEA GIRT, N.J. (AP) — The federal government gave a key approval Tuesday to an offshore wind farm in New Jersey, even as residents in the town where its power cable would come ashore worry it could go through underground toxic waste that's still being cleaned up. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cyber Leaders Exchange 2024: ONCD’s Harry Wingo on lowering barriers to good-paying cyber jobs
item.description: The Office of the National Cyber Director wants to fill thousands of open federal jobs under its Service for America initiative. The post Cyber Leaders Exchange 2024: ONCD’s Harry Wingo on lowering barriers to good-paying cyber jobs first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network
